title: Podman socket (user) not working
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman socket activation not working as advertised.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Enable (--user) podman.socket

2. Try to connect

3. Doesn't work

**Describe the results you received:**

~~~
➜  ~ systemctl --user status podman.socket
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
     Loaded: loaded (/home/jreimann/.config/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; vendor preset: disabled)
     Active: active (listening) since Tue 2021-11-16 13:42:34 CET; 2 weeks 2 days ago
   Triggers: ● podman.service
       Docs: man:podman-api(1)
     Listen: /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock (Stream)
     CGroup: /user.slice/user-110147.slice/user@110147.service/app.slice/podman.socket

Nov 16 13:42:34 brocken systemd[2272]: Listening on Podman API Socket.
➜  ~ ls /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock
ls: cannot access '/run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock': No such file or directory
➜  ~ curl -v --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock http://localhost/version
*   Trying /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock:0...
* Immediate connect fail for /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock: No such file or directory
* Closing connection 0
curl: (7) Couldn't connect to server
~~~

**Describe the results you expected:**

Works.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.1
API Version:  3.4.1
Go Version:   go1.16.8
Built:        Wed Oct 20 16:31:56 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
```
➜  ~ systemctl --user status podman.socket
[...]
     Listen: /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock (Stream)
[...]
➜  ~ ls /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock
ls: cannot access '/run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock': No such file or directory
```

That doesn't make sense to me.  Why is systemd reporting the socket to be active when it's not?

FWIW, I cannot reproduce on my workstation, neither on a fresh F35 server VM.

@ctron, could you retry after a reboot?

> @ctron, could you retry after a reboot?

I just did. It was there.

Then I configured IntelliJ again to make use of it. And it went away.

Re-starting `podman.socket` brings it back.

Makes it more weird :) Not sure what causes that behavior.

Thanks for checking.

@giuseppe @Luap99 do you have any suspicion?

I just did a bit more testing. When I restart the service/socket, it is present. When I then connect with IntellIJ I see the following in the user's journal:

~~~
Dec 07 11:57:58 brocken systemd[2661]: Starting Podman API Service...
Dec 07 11:57:58 brocken systemd[2661]: Started podman-1545518.scope.
Dec 07 11:58:03 brocken systemd[2661]: Finished Podman API Service.
~~~

After that, the socket is gone.

Something is removing this socket file. I cannot reproduce.

Can you test after starting the socket `podman --remote info` wait at least 5 seconds after that the service should be stopped, now check if the socket file is there.

Maybe IntellIJ is causing this problem?

Hm, that doesn't look like an IntelliJ issue:

~~~
➜  ~ ls /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock
ls: cannot access '/run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock': No such file or directory
➜  ~ systemctl --user restart podman.socket
➜  ~ ls /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock
/run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock
➜  ~ podman --remote info
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v3.4.2/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory
➜  ~ ls /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock
ls: cannot access '/run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock': No such file or directory
~~~

Around 20 seconds later, still:

~~~
➜  ~ ls /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock
ls: cannot access '/run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock': No such file or directory
~~~
Can you create another user and try there.

> What does `systemctl status --user podman.sock` show?

~~~
➜  ~ systemctl status --user podman.sock
Unit podman.sock.service could not be found.
~~~

I'm sorry should have been socket.
 systemctl status --user podman.socket

Ah ok :)

~~~
➜  ~ systemctl status --user podman.socket
● podman.socket - Podman API Socket
     Loaded: loaded (/home/jreimann/.config/systemd/user/podman.socket; enabled; vendor preset: disabled)
     Active: active (listening) since Tue 2021-12-07 15:05:09 CET; 1 day 2h ago
   Triggers: ● podman.service
       Docs: man:podman-api(1)
     Listen: /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock (Stream)
     CGroup: /user.slice/user-110147.slice/user@110147.service/app.slice/podman.socket

Dec 07 15:05:09 brocken systemd[2661]: Listening on Podman API Socket.
~~~

So that says the systemd should be listening on /run/user/110147/podman/podman.sock  and the socket should exist.  If the socket does not exists, then something must have removed it, without systemd doing it. Anyways I would bet that something on your system is cleaning this.  If the socket is disapearing, then Podman is not involved.
But just starting `podman --remote info` triggers this behavior. So only `systemd` and `podman` are involved here.

Ok, I just did a quick peek at the code:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/02eaebda46418d6a69a54a08066080fa24f38ebe/cmd/podman/system/service.go#L94-L96

And at the systemd docs: https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd.socket.html

> Defaults to no. […] A daemon listening on an AF_UNIX socket may, but does not need to, call close(2) on the received socket before exiting. However, it must not unlink the socket from a file system.

And the podman unit doesn't set any value: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/contrib/systemd/system/podman.socket

So I guess podman doesn't behave as systemd expects it. Maybe I overlooked something. I will try to set `Accept=yes` and see if that makes any difference.

Ok, I played a bit more with that. And got it resolved by:

~~~
systemctl stop podman.socket
systemctl stop podman.service
rm ~/.config/systemd/user/podman.*
systemctl --user daemon-reload
systemctl --user enable --now podman.socket
~~~

In the original file (under /home/user) the service (not the socket) unit was different than the one under `/usr/lib/`. By deleting and re-creating it, systemd created a symlink (instead of making a copy) to the one under `/usr/lib`. Which might have changed over time, I don't know.

Turns out, the issue is resolved! One only needs to ensure that there are no stale .socket AND .service files.

How would you suggest to make it work also when socket path is given? I'm having very similar issue, but I need to use different path for the socket file. So I created my own .socket and .service unit files with .service having ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman $LOGGING system service --time=0 unix:///my/path. I made it work once, but unfortunately podman does not create the socket file.
